evals.prompt: Use the openalex skill to find papers about open government data published after 2022
evals.expected_output: A readable list of results showing title, year, and citation count. curl uses --data-urlencode, includes api_key, uses select= and jq. Single-line curl.
expectations: Output includes at least 5 paper titles as readable text (not raw JSON)
Output includes publication year for each paper
Output includes citation count for each paper
curl command uses --data-urlencode for parameters (not manual URL concatenation)
api_key parameter included in the request
select= parameter used to restrict returned fields
jq used to format/parse output

evals.prompt: Find all academic papers that have 'open data' in the title, published between 2020 and 2024
evals.expected_output: Uses filter=title.search:"open data" (not a standalone title.search= parameter). Results show title, year, citations.
expectations: Output includes paper titles with 'open data' in the title
Date range 2020-2024 is respected in the results
Uses title.search inside filter= parameter (not as a standalone query parameter)
title.search value is NOT passed as a standalone --data-urlencode 'title.search=...' parameter

evals.prompt: Find all papers published by researcher Ludo Waltman on bibliometrics
evals.expected_output: Performs two-step lookup: first resolves author name to OpenAlex ID, then uses that ID in a filter. Does NOT filter by name string directly.
expectations: Output includes papers by Ludo Waltman related to bibliometrics
Results include VOSviewer paper (Waltman's most cited bibliometrics work)
Uses OpenAlex /authors endpoint to find author ID before filtering works
Works are filtered by author ID (authorships.author.id:...) not by name string

evals.prompt: Get title, year, and citation count for these three papers: DOI 10.1038/s41597-019-0033-6, 10.1371/journal.pone.0228621, and 10.1016/j.dib.2020.105824
evals.expected_output: Uses a single pipe-separated filter (doi:URL1|URL2|URL3) rather than three sequential curl calls. Results show title, year, citations.
expectations: All three papers appear in the output
Output includes title, year, and citation count for each paper
Uses a single batch request with pipe-separated DOIs (doi:...|...|...) not three separate curl calls
Nobel laureates paper (10.1038/s41597-019-0033-6) is identified with 79+ citations

evals.prompt: How many open access articles on climate change have been published per year from 2018 to 2023?
evals.expected_output: Uses group_by=publication_year. Does not use nested field paths in select=. Output shows year and count.
expectations: Output shows article counts per year from 2018 to 2023
Years 2018 through 2023 are all represented in the output
Uses group_by=publication_year to aggregate (not manual counting across pages)
Counts show an increasing trend (2023 > 2018)

evals.prompt: Search for papers about GDPR and data protection published after 2018 and save the results as a CSV with title, year, and DOI
evals.expected_output: Uses jq with @csv and -r flag. Includes // "" null guard for doi field. Header row matches data columns. Saves to a .csv file.
expectations: A CSV file is created with at least 10 rows of paper data
CSV has a header row with title, year, and doi columns
CSV uses proper quoting (jq @csv format)
DOI field uses null guard (// "") to avoid jq errors on null values
